snippets.title: Image augmentation with torchvision transforms
snippets.code:
from torchvision import transforms
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader
from torchvision.datasets import ImageFolder

train_tf = transforms.Compose([
    transforms.RandomResizedCrop(224, scale=(0.8, 1.0)),
    transforms.RandomHorizontalFlip(p=0.5),
    transforms.ColorJitter(brightness=0.2, contrast=0.2),
    transforms.ToTensor(),
])

val_tf = transforms.Compose([
    transforms.Resize(256),
    transforms.CenterCrop(224),
    transforms.ToTensor(),
])

train_loader = DataLoader(ImageFolder("data/train", train_tf), batch_size=32, shuffle=True)
val_loader = DataLoader(ImageFolder("data/val", val_tf), batch_size=32)
snippets.explanation: Random transforms only on training; validation uses fixed resize/center crop so metrics reflect deployment-like inputs.

snippets.title: On-the-fly augmentation in a Keras image pipeline
snippets.code:
from tensorflow.keras.preprocessing.image import ImageDataGenerator

train_gen = ImageDataGenerator(
    rotation_range=15,
    width_shift_range=0.1,
    height_shift_range=0.1,
    horizontal_flip=True,
    zoom_range=0.1,
)
val_gen = ImageDataGenerator()  # no random augmentation

train_flow = train_gen.flow_from_directory("data/train", target_size=(224, 224), batch_size=32)
val_flow = val_gen.flow_from_directory("data/val", target_size=(224, 224), batch_size=32)
snippets.explanation: Each epoch sees new random variants of training images; validation generator leaves pixels unchanged.

snippets.title: Simple text synonym replacement (conceptual)
snippets.explanation: Replace a fraction of tokens with synonyms to vary surface form; verify replacements do not change the label (e.g., sentiment).

snippets.title: Tabular training noise with NumPy
snippets.code:
import numpy as np

def add_gaussian_noise(X, scale=0.02, rng=None):
    rng = rng or np.random.default_rng()
    sigma = scale * np.std(X, axis=0, keepdims=True)
    return X + rng.normal(0, sigma, size=X.shape)

X_batch_noisy = add_gaussian_noise(X_batch, scale=0.02)
model.partial_fit(X_batch_noisy, y_batch)  # pseudo online step
snippets.explanation: Inject small zero-mean noise per feature during training only; scale relative to column std to avoid destroying signal.

snippets.title: SMOTE oversampling with imbalanced-learn (usage only)
snippets.code:
from imblearn.over_sampling import SMOTE
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, stratify=y, test_size=0.2)

smote = SMOTE(random_state=0)
X_res, y_res = smote.fit_resample(X_train, y_train)

# Train classifier on X_res, y_res; evaluate on untouched X_test, y_test
snippets.explanation: SMOTE synthesizes minority-class rows from neighbors — apply only on training split, never on validation or test.
